title: linaria depauperata var. hegelmaieri
intro: Linaria depauperata var. hegelmaieri, also known as the dwarf toadflax, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
